page: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with a minimal memory capacity of 1.0 GB, yielding 0.5 GB of RAM per core. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2701.19. However, multi-core performance under stress-ng is weak, scoring the same 2701.19 and falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is poor at 140.76 GB/sec. Other benchmarks, including Passmark CPU score (3130.58), Passmark CPU encryption (2150.03), and Redis SET operations (2.25 million operations per second), consistently rank in the lower tiers. Static web serving throughput reaches 2.77 GB/sec.

This server type is designed for low-cost, burstable workloads where continuous high CPU utilization is not required. The low memory allocation of 1.0 GB and lack of local storage represent significant resource constraints, making it highly cost-efficient for lightweight tasks but unsuitable for memory-intensive applications or heavy database operations. It is best suited for small-scale utility services, low-traffic web servers, development or testing environments, and simple background tasks that experience periodic bursts in CPU demand.
description: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1.0 GB of memory. Operating on the x86_64 architecture via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 3130.58 and poor memory bandwidth. Given its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and burstable CPU allocation, this server is a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. It is best suited for small utility services, development environments, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, designed for low-cost, lightweight workloads with bursty CPU demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 2 vCPUs and 1.0 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable virtual machine with AMD EPYC processing, optimized for lightweight utility tasks and low-traffic web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Configured with 2 vCPUs and a compact 1.0 GB of system memory
Burstable CPU allocation suitable for variable, non-continuous workloads
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasted by weak multi-core results
No local storage included, but supports premium storage options
Best suited for lightweight utility services, testing, and low-traffic web servers
